item.title: UBS Boss Ermotti Warns Investors on Iran Over-Confidence
item.description: UBS Group AG Chief Executive Officer Sergio Ermotti said that financial markets are at risk of over-optimism regarding the Iran conflict.

item.title: Hong Kong Has Widest Trade Deficit Since 1952 in Echo of AI Boom
item.description: Hong Kong ran its widest trade deficit in at least 74 years as imports surged, a likely spillover from the disruptions caused by the war in the Middle East and a global investment boom in artificial intelligence that’s driving demand for tech products.

item.title: Haleon’s First-Quarter Sales Dragged Down by Weak Cold Season
item.description: Haleon Plc’s first quarter was weaker than expected, hurt by lackluster demand for over-the-counter cold and flu medicines at the start of the year.

item.title: Aston Martin Posts Another Loss as Carmaker’s Debt Swells
item.description: Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc reported another quarterly loss as the embattled carmaker struggles to engineer a turnaround under billionaire Lawrence Stroll.

item.title: Carlsberg Sales Volumes Rise as Alcohol-Free Push Gains Traction
item.description: Carlsberg A/S started the year with positive sales volumes in all regions, a sign the Danish brewer’s strategy to diversify into alcohol-free drinks is working in spite of disruptions from the Iran war.

item.title: GSK Profit Beats Estimates on HIV, Cancer Medicines
item.description: GSK Plc’s profit was higher than analysts expected in the first quarter, helped by the drugmaker’s specialty medicines for HIV, cancer and immune diseases.

item.title: Founder Gets 9-Year Jail Term in $300 Million Indonesia Case
item.description: Indonesia handed eFishery founder Gibran Huzaifah a nine-year prison sentence on Wednesday, concluding a high-profile $300 million financial scandal that destroyed one of Southeast Asia’s most celebrated startups.

item.title: Amadeus Agrees to Buy Idemia Unit for €1.2 Billion
item.description: Amadeus IT SA said it has agreed to buy the public security arm of Advent-backed biometrics technology provider Idemia for €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion).

item.title: Central Banks ‘Scoop Up a Load’ of Gold in Bumpy First Quarter
item.description: Central banks added gold holdings at the fastest pace in more than a year in the first quarter, as a slump in prices encouraged a wave of buying that more than offset sales by a handful of institutions.

item.title: Distressed UK Firms Pile Up as War Enters Third Month, Report Says
item.description: UK companies faced a sharp jump in “critical” financial distress at the start of 2026, underscoring the mounting pressure that rising costs and weak consumer demand are placing on businesses as the war in the Middle East enters its third month.

item.title: Finland’s Kone Agrees to Buy Rival TK Elevator for €29.4 Billion
item.description: Kone Oyj agreed to acquire TK Elevator for €29.4 billion ($34.4 billion) including debt, in what will be one of Europe’s biggest-ever private equity exits.

item.title: Sri Lanka Discloses New Payment Lapse Days After Hack Revelation
item.description: Sri Lanka’s government has disclosed a payment lapse involving funds due to the US Post, days after revealing a separate cyberattack, raising fresh questions about its financial safeguards as it continues to recover from an unprecedented default.

item.title: James Murdoch-Backed Allen Career Institute Is Said to Consider India IPO
item.description: Allen Career Institute, an Indian education services company backed by James Murdoch and ex-Disney executive Uday Shankar, is considering an initial public offering in Mumbai, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: FTSE 100 Futures, Pound Fall as Lloyds and Astra Report Earnings
Deutsche Bank CFO Says Trading Business Started Well in April
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG saw its fixed income and currencies trading business start “constructively” in the second quarter after some jitters in March, Chief Financial Officer Raja Akram said in an interview.

item.title: Volvo Car Earnings Slip on Tougher US, China Competition
item.description: Volvo Car AB’s earnings declined in the first quarter as the Sweden-based automaker grapples with waning electric-vehicle sales in the US and rising competition in China.

item.title: Pungent Fruit Gets Influencer Backing as Thailand Faces Glut
item.description: Thailand is turning to livestreamers to clear a looming durian glut, slashing prices as weaker Chinese demand threatens its biggest export market.

item.title: China Rare Earth Shares Rally as Beijing Targets Output Breaches
item.description: Shares in China’s major rare earths producers advanced after the government unveiled detailed penalties for unauthorized production, marking a fresh effort to bolster Beijing’s “total control” regime for the geopolitically pivotal industry.

item.title: ECB Finds No Clear Case to Hike Yet on Lane’s Data Dashboard
item.description: The array of data the European Central Bank is scrutinizing to pinpoint economic damage from the Iran war is signaling that there’s no cast-iron case to lift interest rates just yet.

item.title: Copper Snaps Four-Day Decline on China Buying Ahead of Holiday
item.description: Copper advanced as some Chinese buyers replenished stockpiles ahead of a holiday, although concerns about global growth persisted with no immediate sign of a resolution to the Iran war.

item.title: Chip Supply Chains Risk Being Next Casualty of War
item.description: The question is whether companies have enough stockpiles on hand to ride out the blockade.

item.title: China Fighter Jet Giant’s Sales Surge After India-Pakistan Clash
item.description: China’s AVIC Chengdu Aircraft Co., maker of the fighter jets that rose to fame in the India-Pakistan conflict last year, posted record profit in 2025 and saw first-quarter sales nearly double.

item.title: Chinese Truckmaker XCMG Auto Is Said to Consider $500 Million Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Chinese truckmaker Xuzhou XCMG Automobile Manufacturing Co. is considering listing shares in Hong Kong, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Netherlands’ Plan for Tax on Wealth Sparks Fierce Opposition
item.description: An investment tax has been unpopular with wealthy people in the Netherlands for years, but critics say a planned revamp is even worse.

item.title: Swiss Population Cap Gets First Majority Backing in Voter Poll
item.description: A proposal to cap Switzerland’s population at 10 million drew support of 52% of respondents in the first such poll showing a majority for the idea.

item.title: Ukraine Faces Risk of Tougher EU Loan Terms to Get Aid Payouts
item.description: The European Union is considering imposing stricter conditions on its €90 billion ($105 billion) loan to Ukraine, making some of the payouts dependent on the introduction of an unpopular tax change for businesses, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: China Changes Agriculture Chief for Second Time in Two Years
item.description: China has removed Han Jun as the most senior Communist Party official at the agriculture ministry, a sudden move that marks the second such reshuffle in under two years.

item.title: Iron Ore Advances as Rising Costs Offset BHP-CMRG Resolution
item.description: Iron ore rose toward a one-month high, despite a resolution of a dispute between BHP Group and China Mineral Resources Group Co. that had supported prices by putting some supply at risk.

item.title: Indian Small Caps Set for Best Month Since 2014 After Rare Rally
item.description: India’s small-cap stocks are on track for their best month in 12 years, as improved earnings outlook and more attractive valuations draw investors back.

item.title: Australia’s New South Wales to Open New Gas Exploration Sites for First Time in a Decade
item.description: Australia’s New South Wales state will open new areas for gas exploration for the first time in more than a decade, to shore up energy security ahead of shortfalls on the country’s populous east coast.
